records.ety: [Pref. dis- + agree: cf. F. dsagrer to displease.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disagreed (ds`*grēd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Disagreeing.]

defs: To fail to accord; not to agree; to lack harmony; to differ; to be unlike; to be at variance.
quotes: They reject the plainest sense of Scripture, because it seems to disagree with what they call reason.

defs: To differ in opinion; to hold discordant views; to be at controversy; to quarrel.
quotes: Who shall decide, when doctors disagree?

defs: To be unsuited; to have unfitness; as, medicine sometimes disagrees with the patient; food often disagrees with the stomach or the taste.
notes: ☞ Usually followed by with, sometimes by to, rarely by from; as, I disagree to your proposal.
